page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6t.26xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 104 dedicated physical cores and 104 vCPUs, utilizing a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The instance is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. It includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.85 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from other tenants. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of dedicated physical cores relative to memory makes this instance suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage.

The hardware profile of the ecs.c6t.26xlarge makes it appropriate for high-performance computing, batch processing, scientific modeling, and intensive web serving. It is also well-suited for distributed analytics and compute-heavy containerized applications that benefit from high core counts and dedicated CPU execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6t.26xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 104 dedicated physical cores and 104 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is configured with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing 1.85 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation and high core count, the ecs.c6t.26xlarge offers consistent processing power for compute-intensive tasks. It is designed for workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, scientific simulations, and large-scale web serving where dedicated CPU resources are critical and local storage is not a primary requirement.
